title: Search Trees: Removal (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 6). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the student selects y incorrectly, base the splicing marks on whether they splice out their own y consistently.
This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is delete(Node root, Node delete); the Python function is delete(root, delete). Both are correct.

The algorithm must follow the lecture method using TREE-MINIMUM: if the node to delete has two children, find its in-order successor using treeMinimum(delete.right), copy the successor's key, and remove the successor instead.
The node class has fields: key (int), left (Node), right (Node), and parent (Node).

rubric_points.name: Selecting y: which node to physically remove
rubric_points.target: y is set to delete when delete has at most one child; y is set to treeMinimum(delete.right) when delete has two children.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: y is always set to delete regardless of how many children it has, or no selection logic is present.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: The case split is present but partially incorrect: for example, the condition for detecting two children is wrong, or the successor is found using the wrong subtree (e.g., treeMinimum of the left subtree instead of the right).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: y is correctly set to delete when delete has zero or one child, and to treeMinimum(delete.right) when delete has two children.

rubric_points.name: treeMinimum helper
rubric_points.target: treeMinimum walks left from its input node until reaching a node with no left child, and returns that node.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: treeMinimum is absent, or always returns the input node without any traversal.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: treeMinimum traverses the tree but in the wrong direction (e.g., goes right instead of left), or terminates under an incorrect condition.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: treeMinimum walks left (n = n.left) until n.left is null and returns n, correctly finding the in-order minimum.

rubric_points.name: Finding x: the child of y to promote
rubric_points.target: x is identified as y's non-null child (y.left if non-null, otherwise y.right), which may be null if y is a leaf.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: x is not identified; the algorithm proceeds without determining which child of y (if any) should take y's place.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: x is assigned but the logic is incorrect: for example, always takes y.left even when it is null, or does not handle the case where y is a leaf (x should be null).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: x is set to y.left when y.left is non-null, and to y.right otherwise (which may be null if y is a leaf), correctly identifying the child to promote.

rubric_points.name: Splicing y out of the tree
rubric_points.target: y is removed by updating x.parent to y.parent (when x is non-null), and updating y.parent's child pointer (or root) to x.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: y.parent's child pointers are not updated; y remains linked in the tree after deletion.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: y.parent's left or right pointer is redirected to x, but x.parent is not updated (or vice versa), or the root replacement case (y.parent == null) is missing.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: x.parent is set to y.parent (when x is non-null); y.parent's left or right pointer is redirected to x based on which child y was; and the root replacement case (y.parent == null → root = x) is handled.

rubric_points.name: Copying the key when y is the successor
rubric_points.target: When y is the in-order successor (y != delete), delete.key is updated to y.key so that the logical node is removed while the physical successor node is spliced out.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: The key is never copied, leaving delete.key unchanged when the successor was used; the node with delete.key remains in the tree.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A key copy is attempted but without checking y != delete, potentially overwriting the key even in the direct deletion case (when y == delete).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: delete.key = y.key is executed only when y != delete, correctly replacing the logical node's key with the successor's key.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: The method returns a valid BST after deletion for all cases: leaf, one child, two children, and root deletion.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Method returns null or produces an invalid BST for most inputs.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Deletion works for simple cases (leaf or single-child node) but fails for two-children deletion, or fails when the node to delete is the root.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: BST property is preserved after deletion for all cases including two-children deletion, root deletion, and leaf deletion. Correct root is returned in all cases.

buckets.label: Internally consistent but incorrect final result
buckets.description: Logic follows consistently from the student's own selection and splicing approach, but the resulting tree is incorrect due to a systematic error (e.g., consistently wrong successor selection or missing pointer update).
